import asyncio
import os
import rich
import subprocess
import sys
import humanize

from collections import Counter
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Set
from lsp.logs import get_logger, logging_fwk_to_console
from lsp.storage import load_all_datasets, Datasets
from lsp.chunks.chunker import get_file_stat
from lsp.fs import load_rag_config, Config
from lsp.filetypes import EXTENSION_TO_FILETYPE
from index.stale import warn_about_stale_files

logger = get_logger("validator")


class DatasetsValidator:

    def __init__(self, datasets: Datasets):
        self.datasets = datasets
        self.any_problems = False

    def validate_datasets(self):
        for dataset in self.datasets.all_datasets.values():
            any_problem_with_this_dataset = False

            # * compare # vectors to # IDs
            num_vectors_based_on_ntotal = dataset.index_view.num_vectors()
            ids = dataset.index_view.ids
            if len(ids) != num_vectors_based_on_ntotal:
                logger.error(f"{len(ids)=} != {num_vectors_based_on_ntotal=}")
                any_problem_with_this_dataset = True

            # * test for duplicate IDs
            duplicate_ids = list(dataset.index_view._check_for_duplicate_ids())
            for id, count in duplicate_ids:
                if count <= 1:
                    continue
                any_problem_with_this_dataset = True
                chunk = self.datasets.get_chunk_by_faiss_id(id)
                if chunk is None:
                    logger.error(f"Duplicate ID found: {id} - {count}x - missing chunk too")
                else:
                    logger.error(f"Duplicate ID found: {id} with {count}x - {chunk.file} L{chunk.base0.start_line}-{chunk.base0.end_line}")

            # * compare chunk counts vs ID counts
            num_unique_ids_based_on_ids = len(duplicate_ids)
            num_chunks_based_on_chunks = sum([len(file) for file in dataset.chunks_by_file.values()])

            if num_unique_ids_based_on_ids != num_chunks_based_on_chunks:
                any_problem_with_this_dataset = True
                logger.error(f"chunk count mismatch: {num_unique_ids_based_on_ids=} != {num_chunks_based_on_chunks=}")

            num_vectors_based_on_ids = sum([count for _, count in duplicate_ids])
            if num_vectors_based_on_ntotal != num_vectors_based_on_ids:
                any_problem_with_this_dataset = True
                logger.error(f"vectors count mismatch: {num_vectors_based_on_ntotal=} != {num_vectors_based_on_ids=}")

            if any_problem_with_this_dataset:
                logger.info(f"{num_vectors_based_on_ntotal=}")
                logger.info(f'{num_vectors_based_on_ids=} {num_unique_ids_based_on_ids=}')
                logger.info(f'{num_chunks_based_on_chunks=}')

            self.any_problems = self.any_problems or any_problem_with_this_dataset

        if self.any_problems:
            logger.error("[bold red]AT LEAST ONE PROBLEM DISCOVERED")
        else:
            logger.debug("[bold green]ALL CHECKS PASS!")

    def warn_about_unindexed_filetypes(self, datasets: Datasets) -> None:
        """Warn about filetypes with many files that aren't indexed."""

        def find_filetypes_in_cwd():
            fd_command = ["fd", "--type", "file"]
            out = subprocess.check_output(fd_command, text=True)

            filetype_counts = Counter()
            for file_path in out.splitlines():
                ext = Path(file_path).suffix.lower().lstrip(".")
                if ext:
                    filetype = EXTENSION_TO_FILETYPE.get(ext, ext)
                    filetype_counts[filetype] += 1
            return filetype_counts

        filetype_counts = find_filetypes_in_cwd()

        EXTENSION_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 10
        frequent_filetypes = {ft for ft, count in filetype_counts.items() if count > EXTENSION_COUNT_THRESHOLD}
        indexed_filetypes = datasets.get_indexed_filetypes()
        missing_filetypes = frequent_filetypes - indexed_filetypes

        if missing_filetypes:
            missing_counts = {ft: filetype_counts[ft] for ft in missing_filetypes}
            logger.debug("Found prominent, unindexed filetypes: " + ", ".join( \
                f"{ft}={count}" for ft, count in missing_counts.items()))
        else:
            logger.debug("All good, no missing filetypes, you lucky motherf***er")

    def compare_config_vs_indexed_filetypes(self, datasets: Datasets, config: Config) -> None:
        """Compare configured filetypes against what's actually indexed on disk."""

        present_filetypes = set(datasets.all_datasets.keys())
        configured_filetypes = set(config.include)

        extra_filetypes = present_filetypes - configured_filetypes
        if extra_filetypes:
            self.any_problems = True
            logger.error( \
                "[bold white on red]Vestigial datasets (filetypes that aren't indexed): "
                + ", ".join(sorted(extra_filetypes))
            )

        missing_filetypes = configured_filetypes - present_filetypes
        if missing_filetypes:
            self.any_problems = True
            logger.error( \
                "[bold white on red]Missing datasets (configured filetypes): "
                + ", ".join(sorted(missing_filetypes))
            )


async def main():
    # usage:
    #   python3 -m index.validate $(_repo_root)/.rag

    logging_fwk_to_console(level="DEBUG")

    rag_dir = Path(sys.argv[1])
    ds = load_all_datasets(rag_dir)

    validator = DatasetsValidator(ds)
    validator.validate_datasets()
    validator.warn_about_unindexed_filetypes(ds)

    root_dir = rag_dir.parent
    warn_about_stale_files(ds, root_dir)

    config = await load_rag_config(root_dir)
    validator.compare_config_vs_indexed_filetypes(ds, config)

    if validator.any_problems:
        sys.exit(1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

datasets: Datasets

@attrs.define
class LSPSemanticGrepResult:
    """ Either return matches OR an error string, nothing else matters."""
    matches: list = []
    error: str | None = None

class LSPResponseErrors:
    NO_RAG_DIR = "No .rag dir"
    CANCELLED = "Client cancelled query"

async def handle_semantic_grep_ls_command(args: LSPSemanticGrepRequest) -> LSPSemanticGrepResult:
    stopper = create_stopper(args.msgId)
    try:
        if fs.is_no_rag_dir():
            return LSPSemanticGrepResult(error=LSPResponseErrors.NO_RAG_DIR)

        # TODO! REVIEW the ASYNC (i.e. for file ops? or other async capable ops)

        query = args.query
        if query is None or len(query) == 0:
            logger.info("No query provided")
            return LSPSemanticGrepResult(error="No query provided")

        stopper.throw_if_stopped()

        matches = await semantic_grep(
            args=args,
            datasets=datasets,
            stopper=stopper,
        )

        return LSPSemanticGrepResult(matches=matches)
    finally:
        remove_stopper(args.msgId)

async def update_file_from_pygls_doc(lsp_doc: TextDocument, options: RAGChunkerOptions):
    file_path = Path(lsp_doc.path)

    with logger.timer(f"update_file {fs.get_loggable_path(file_path)}"):
        hash = get_file_hash_from_lines(lsp_doc.lines)
        # FYI you can check hash for changes, but remember this is off of disk and you only update that on commit
        #   so really there's no point to ask if changed, b/c only going to be saving materially if altering it
        #   in which case it's always gonna look altered at least LS side
        new_chunks = build_chunks_from_lines(file_path, hash, lsp_doc.lines, options)  # PRN await
        await datasets.update_file(file_path, new_chunks)

{"command_line":"cat > /tmp/patch_indexer.py << 'PYEOF'
import re

filepath = "~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/rag/indexer.py"

with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Fix the _build_fd_glob_for_filetype method
old_method = '''    def _build_fd_glob_for_filetype(self, filetype: str) -> str:
        """Build an fd glob pattern matching all extensions for a given filetype.

        e.g. filetype "yaml" → ".*\\.(yaml|yml)$"
             filetype "py" → ".*\\.py$"
             filetype "javascript" → ".*\\.(js|jsx)$"
        """
        extensions = get_extensions_for_filetype(filetype)
        if len(extensions) == 1:
            return f".*\\\\\\\\.{extensions[0]}$"
        # Multiple extensions → alternation group
        ext_group = "|".join(sorted(extensions))
        return f".*\\\\\\\\.({ext_group})$"'''

new_method = '''    def _build_fd_glob_for_filetype(self, filetype: str) -> str:
        """Build an fd glob pattern matching all extensions for a given filetype.

        e.g. filetype "yaml" → ".*\\.(yaml|yml)$"
             filetype "py" → ".*\\.py$"
             filetype "javascript" → ".*\\.(js|jsx)$"
        """
        extensions = get_extensions_for_filetype(filetype)
        ext_list = sorted(extensions)
        if len(ext_list) == 1:
            return f".*\\\\.{ext_list[0]}$"
        # Multiple extensions → alternation group
        ext_group = "|".join(ext_list)
        return f".*\\\\.({ext_group})$"'''

content = content.replace(old_method, new_method)

with open(filepath, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)

print("Patched")
PYEOF

        """Build an fd glob pattern matching all extensions for a given filetype.

        e.g. filetype "yaml" → ".*\\.(yaml|yml)$"
             filetype "py" → ".*\\.py$"
             filetype "javascript" → ".*\\.(js|jsx)$"
        """
        extensions = get_extensions_for_filetype(filetype)
        if len(extensions) == 1:
            return f".*\\\\.{extensions[0]}$"
        # Multiple extensions → alternation group
        ext_group = "|".join(sorted(extensions))
        return f".*\\\\.({ext_group})$"

    def get_files_diff(self, filetype: str, prior_stat_by_path: dict[str, FileStat]) -> FilesDiff:
        """Split files into: changed (added/updated), unchanged, deleted"""

"}]}

filepath = "~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/rag/indexer.py"

for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if 'def _build_fd_glob_for_filetype' in line and not method_done:
        in_method = True
        new_lines.append(line)
        continue

        # Replace this line and the next few lines
        new_lines.append('        extensions = sorted(get_extensions_for_filetype(filetype))\n')
        continue
    
    if in_method and 'if len(extensions) == 1:' in line:
        # Already handled by sorting above
        new_lines.append(line)
        continue

        """Split files into: changed (added/updated), unchanged, deleted"""

        # * ignored files

new_get_files_diff = '''    def get_files_diff(self, filetype: str, prior_stat_by_path: dict[str, FileStat]) -> FilesDiff:
        """Split files into: changed (added/updated), unchanged, deleted"""

        # * Find files with matching extensions via fd
        glob_pattern = self._build_fd_glob_for_filetype(filetype)
        cmd_ext = ["fd", glob_pattern, str(self.source_code_dir), "--absolute-path", "--type", "f"]
        logger.debug(f"extension files cmd: {cmd_ext}")
        result_ext = subprocess.run(
            cmd_ext,
            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
            text=True,
            check=True,
        )
        current_path_strs = set(result_ext.stdout.strip().splitlines())

        # * Also find extensionless files that map to this filetype
        #   (Makefile → make, deploy → shell via shebang, etc.)
        cmd_all = ["fd", ".", str(self.source_code_dir), "--absolute-path", "--type", "f"]
        logger.debug(f"all files cmd: {cmd_all}")
        result_all = subprocess.run(
            cmd_all,

            text=True,
            check=True,
        )
        all_files = result_all.stdout.strip().splitlines()

        for file_path_str in all_files:

            resolved = resolve_filetype(file_path_str)
            if resolved == filetype:

            logger.info(f"Ignoring files ({len(ignored_path_strs)}):\n    {'\n    '.join(ignored_path_strs)}")
        current_path_strs -= ignored_path_strs'''

content = content.replace(old_get_files_diff, new_get_files_diff)

with open(filepath, 'w') as f:

print("Patched get_files_diff to handle extensionless files")
PYEOF

{"command_line":"cat > ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/rag/indexer.py << 'PYEOF'
import aiofiles
import asyncio
from lsp.logs import disable_printtmp, get_logger, disable_printtmp
from lsp.inference.client.embedder import get_shape, encode_passages, signal_hotpath_done_in_background

logger = get_logger(__name__)

import argparse
import logging
import subprocess
import sys
import yaml
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional, Set

import faiss
import numpy as np

from pydants import write_json

from lsp.storage import Chunk, FileStat, load_prior_data
from lsp.chunks.chunker import RAGChunkerOptions, build_chunks_from_file, get_file_stat
from lsp.config import Config, load_config
from lsp.ignores import is_ignored_allchecks
from lsp.filetypes import (
    EXTENSION_TO_FILETYPE,
    get_extensions_for_filetype,
    resolve_filetype,
)
from lsp import fs

# constants for subprocess.run for readability
IGNORE_FAILURE = False
STOP_ON_FAILURE = True


@dataclass
class FilesDiff:
    changed: Set[Path]
    deleted: Set[str]
    not_changed: Set[str]


@dataclass
class ProgramArgs:
    verbose: bool
    info: bool
    in_githook: bool
    rebuild: bool
    level: int
    only_filetype: str | None = None


def trash_dir(directory):
    directory = Path(directory)
    if not directory.exists():
        return
    subprocess.run(["trash", directory], check=IGNORE_FAILURE)


class IncrementalRAGIndexer:

    def __init__(
        self,
        dot_rag_dir: Path,
        source_code_dir: Path,
        options: RAGChunkerOptions,
        program_args: ProgramArgs,
        config: Config,
    ):
        self.options = options
        self.dot_rag_dir = Path(dot_rag_dir)
        self.source_code_dir = Path(source_code_dir)
        self.program_args = program_args
        self.config = config

    async def main(self):
        if not self.config.enabled:
            logger.warn(f"RAG indexing disabled in {self.source_code_dir / '.rag.yaml'}, ")
            return

        included = self.config.include
        if self.program_args and self.program_args.only_filetype:
            included = [self.program_args.only_filetype]
            logger.info(f"Indexing only filetype: {included[0]}")

        for filetype in included:
            await self.build_index(filetype)
        self.flag_unindexed_filetypes_with_file_counts(included)
        self.trash_vestigial_filetype_indexes(set(included))
        await signal_hotpath_done_in_background()

    def trash_vestigial_filetype_indexes(self, configured_filetypes: set[str]):
        rag_dir_dirs = [p for p in self.dot_rag_dir.iterdir() if p.is_dir()]
        indexed_filetypes = {d.name for d in rag_dir_dirs}
        vestigial_filetypes = indexed_filetypes - configured_filetypes

        if not any(vestigial_filetypes):
            return

        logger.warn(f'FYI found {vestigial_filetypes=}, removing...')

        for name in vestigial_filetypes:
            filetype_dir = self.dot_rag_dir / name
            logger.warn(f"Removing vestigial rag dir: {filetype_dir}")
            trash_dir(filetype_dir)

    def flag_unindexed_filetypes_with_file_counts(self, index_filetypes: list[str]):
        """Warn about filetypes present in the repo but not being indexed."""

        cmd = ["fish", "-c", f"fd . {self.source_code_dir} --exclude='\\.ctags\\.d' --exclude='\\.rag' --exec basename"]
        logger.debug("warn cmd", cmd)
        result = subprocess.run(
            cmd,
            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
            text=True,
            check=STOP_ON_FAILURE,
        )
        basenames = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()

        ignore_files = {"ctags", "ctags.d"}

        # Resolve each basename to its filetype
        filetypes: list[str] = []
        for basename in basenames:
            if not basename or basename in ignore_files or "." not in basename:
                continue
            ext = basename.rsplit(".", 1)[-1]
            filetype = EXTENSION_TO_FILETYPE.get(ext, ext)
            filetypes.append(filetype)

        import itertools
        unindexed_filetypes = [(ft, len(list(group))) \
            for ft, group in itertools.groupby(sorted(filetypes)) \
            if ft not in index_filetypes
        ]

        noteworthy_filetypes = [ \
            f"{ft}={count}" \
            for ft, count in unindexed_filetypes \
            if count > 1
        ]

        if noteworthy_filetypes:
            logger.debug(f"Found unindexed filetypes: {' '.join(noteworthy_filetypes)}")

    def _build_fd_glob_for_filetype(self, filetype: str) -> str:
        """Build an fd glob pattern matching all extensions for a given filetype.

        e.g. filetype "yaml" → ".*\\.(yaml|yml)$"
             filetype "py" → ".*\\.py$"
             filetype "javascript" → ".*\\.(js|jsx)$"
        """
        extensions = sorted(get_extensions_for_filetype(filetype))
        if len(extensions) == 1:
            return f".*\\\\.{extensions[0]}$"
        # Multiple extensions → alternation group
        ext_group = "|".join(extensions)
        return f".*\\\\.({ext_group})$"

    def get_files_diff(self, filetype: str, prior_stat_by_path: dict[str, FileStat]) -> FilesDiff:
        """Split files into: changed (added/updated), unchanged, deleted"""

        # * Find files with matching extensions via fd
        glob_pattern = self._build_fd_glob_for_filetype(filetype)
        cmd_ext = ["fd", glob_pattern, str(self.source_code_dir), "--absolute-path", "--type", "f"]
        logger.debug(f"extension files cmd: {cmd_ext}")
        result_ext = subprocess.run(
            cmd_ext,
            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
            text=True,
            check=True,
        )
        current_path_strs = set(result_ext.stdout.strip().splitlines())

        # * Also find extensionless files that map to this filetype
        #   (Makefile → make, deploy → shell via shebang, etc.)
        cmd_all = ["fd", ".", str(self.source_code_dir), "--absolute-path", "--type", "f"]
        logger.debug(f"all files cmd: {cmd_all}")
        result_all = subprocess.run(
            cmd_all,
            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
            text=True,
            check=True,
        )
        all_files = result_all.stdout.strip().splitlines()

        for file_path_str in all_files:
            if file_path_str in current_path_strs:
                continue  # already found via extension
            resolved = resolve_filetype(file_path_str)
            if resolved == filetype:
                current_path_strs.add(file_path_str)
                logger.debug(f"[green]Extensionless file matched filetype {filetype}: {file_path_str}")

        # * ignored files
        ignored_path_strs: Set[str] = set()
        for path_str in current_path_strs:
            if is_ignored_allchecks(path_str, self.config, self.source_code_dir):
                ignored_path_strs.add(path_str)
        if len(ignored_path_strs) > 0:
            logger.info(f"Ignoring files ({len(ignored_path_strs)}):\n    {'\n    '.join(ignored_path_strs)}")
        current_path_strs -= ignored_path_strs

        # * added, modified (aka changed)
        changed_paths: Set[Path] = set()
        for file_path_str in current_path_strs:
            file_path = Path(file_path_str)
            is_new_file = file_path_str not in prior_stat_by_path
            if is_new_file:
                changed_paths.add(file_path)
                logger.debug(f"[green]New file: {file_path}")
            else:
                current_mod_time = file_path.stat().st_mtime
                prior_mod_time = prior_stat_by_path[file_path_str].mtime
                if current_mod_time > prior_mod_time:
                    changed_paths.add(file_path)
                    logger.debug(f"[blue]Modified file: {file_path}")

        prior_path_strs: Set[str] = set(prior_stat_by_path.keys())

        # * deleted
        deleted_path_strs = prior_path_strs - current_path_strs
        for deleted_file in deleted_path_strs:
            logger.debug(f"[red]Deleted file: {deleted_file}")

        # * not changed
        changed_path_strs = set(str(f) for f in changed_paths)
        not_changed_path_strs = prior_path_strs - changed_path_strs - deleted_path_strs

        return FilesDiff(changed_paths, deleted_path_strs, not_changed_path_strs)

    async def update_faiss_index_incrementally(
        self,
        index: Optional[faiss.Index],
        not_changed_chunks_by_file: dict[str, list[Chunk]],
        updated_chunks_by_file: dict[str, list[Chunk]],
    ) -> faiss.Index:
        """Update FAISS index incrementally using IndexIDMap"""

        if index is None:
            shape = await get_shape()
            base_index = faiss.IndexFlatIP(shape)
            index = faiss.IndexIDMap(base_index)

        new_chunks: list[Chunk] = []
        new_faiss_ids: list[int] = []
        for file_chunks in updated_chunks_by_file.values():
            for chunk in file_chunks:
                new_chunks.append(chunk)
                new_faiss_ids.append(chunk.faiss_id)

        keep_ids = []
        logger.pp_debug("keep_ids - before", keep_ids)
        for _, file_chunks in not_changed_chunks_by_file.items():
            for chunk in file_chunks:
                keep_ids.append(chunk.faiss_id)
        logger.pp_debug("keep_ids - after", keep_ids)

        keep_selector = faiss.IDSelectorArray(np.array(keep_ids, dtype="int64"))
        not_keep_selector = faiss.IDSelectorNot(keep_selector)
        index.remove_ids(not_keep_selector)

        if new_chunks:
            logger.pp_debug("new_faiss_ids", new_faiss_ids)

            with logger.timer("Encode new vectors"):
                passages = [chunk.text for chunk in new_chunks]
                vecs_np = await encode_passages(passages)

            faiss_ids_np = np.array(new_faiss_ids, dtype="int64")

            index.add_with_ids(vecs_np, faiss_ids_np)

        return index

    async def build_index(self, filetype: str = "lua"):
        """Build or update the RAG index incrementally for a given filetype."""

        prior = load_prior_data(self.dot_rag_dir, filetype)

        paths = self.get_files_diff(filetype, prior.stat_by_path)

        logger.pp_debug("paths", paths)

        if not paths.changed and not paths.deleted:
            logger.debug("[green]No changes detected, index is up to date!")
            return

        all_stat_by_path = {path_str: prior.stat_by_path[path_str] for path_str in paths.not_changed}
        not_changed_chunks_by_file = {path_str: prior.chunks_by_file[path_str] for path_str in paths.not_changed}
        logger.info(f'{len(paths.changed)} changed, {len(paths.deleted)} deleted')

        updated_chunks_by_file: dict[str, list[Chunk]] = {}
        for file_path in paths.changed:
            file_path_str = str(file_path)

            stat = get_file_stat(file_path)
            all_stat_by_path[file_path_str] = stat

            chunks = build_chunks_from_file(file_path, stat.hash, self.options)
            updated_chunks_by_file[file_path_str] = chunks

        logger.pp_debug("Deleted chunks", paths.deleted)
        logger.pp_debug("Updated chunks", updated_chunks_by_file)
        logger.pp_debug("NOT changed chunks", not_changed_chunks_by_file)

        if paths.changed or paths.deleted:
            index = await self.update_faiss_index_incrementally(
                prior.index,
                not_changed_chunks_by_file,
                updated_chunks_by_file,
            )
        else:
            index = prior.index

        if index is None:
            return

        # Save everything under the filetype key
        index_dir = self.dot_rag_dir / filetype
        index_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True, parents=True)

        faiss.write_index(index, str(index_dir / "vectors.index"))

        logger.pp_debug("ids: ", prior.index_view.ids)

        with logger.timer("Save chunks"):
            all_chunks_by_file = not_changed_chunks_by_file.copy()
            all_chunks_by_file.update(updated_chunks_by_file)

        with logger.timer("Save chunks"):
            write_json(all_chunks_by_file, index_dir / "chunks.json")

        with logger.timer("Save file stats"):
            write_json(all_stat_by_path, index_dir / "files.json")

        logger.debug(f"[green]Index updated successfully!")
        if paths.changed:
            logger.debug(f"[green]Processed {len(paths.changed)} changed files")
        if paths.deleted:
            logger.debug(f"[green]Removed {len(paths.deleted)} deleted files")


async def main():
    from lsp.logs import logging_fwk_to_console

    def parse_program_args() -> ProgramArgs:
        parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
        parser.add_argument("--verbose", "--debug", action="store_true", help="Enable verbose logging")
        parser.add_argument("--info", action="store_true", help="Enable info logging")
        parser.add_argument("--rebuild", action="store_true", help="Rebuild index")
        parser.add_argument("--githook", action="store_true", help="Run in git hook mode")
        parser.add_argument("--only-filetype", type=str, help="Only process files with the specified filetype")

        args = parser.parse_args()

        program_args = ProgramArgs(
            verbose=args.verbose,
            info=args.info,
            in_githook=args.githook,
            rebuild=args.rebuild,
            level=logging.WARNING,
            only_filetype=args.only_filetype,
        )
        if args.githook:
            level = logging.INFO
        else:
            program_args.level = logging.DEBUG if args.verbose else (logging.INFO if args.info else logging.WARNING)

        return program_args

    disable_printtmp()

    args = parse_program_args()

    logging_fwk_to_console(args.level)

    with logger.timer("Total indexing time"):
        repo_root_dir = fs.get_cwd_repo_root()
        if not repo_root_dir:
            logger.error("[red]No Git repository found in current working directory, cannot build RAG index.")
            sys.exit(1)
        dot_rag_dir = repo_root_dir / ".rag"
        source_code_dir = Path(".").resolve()
        logger.debug(f"[bold]RAG directory: {dot_rag_dir}")
        if args.rebuild:
            trash_dir(dot_rag_dir)

        options = RAGChunkerOptions.ProductionOptions()
        config = await fs.load_rag_config(source_code_dir)
        indexer = IncrementalRAGIndexer(dot_rag_dir, source_code_dir, options, args, config)

        fs.root_path = source_code_dir
        fs.dot_rag_dir = dot_rag_dir

        await indexer.main()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

function.name: run_process
function.arguments:
{"command_line":"cat > ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/rag/lsp/config.py << 'PYEOF'
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
import os
from pathlib import Path

import yaml

from lsp.filetypes import (
    DEFAULT_INCLUDES,
    EXTENSION_TO_FILETYPE,
    FILENAME_TO_FILETYPE,
    get_filetype_for_extension,
    resolve_filetype,
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Config
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

default_ignores: list[str] = []
default_global_languages: list[str] = []  # no defaults b/c if you don't set it, you get all indexed file types (includes)
default_enabled: bool = True


def _normalize_includes(raw_includes: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    """Normalize include list: convert raw extensions to canonical filetypes.

    Handles both old-style raw extensions (js, ts, yml, fish) and
    new-style canonical filetypes (javascript, typescript, yaml, shell).
    Unmapped extensions pass through as-is.
    """
    normalized = []
    for item in raw_includes:
        # If it's already a canonical filetype (not in EXTENSION_TO_FILETYPE as a value's source),
        # check if it maps from an extension
        ext = item.lstrip(".").lower()
        canonical = get_filetype_for_extension(ext)
        normalized.append(canonical)
    return normalized


@dataclass
class Config:
    ignores: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    include: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    global_languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=default_global_languages)
    enabled: bool = field(default=default_enabled)

    @staticmethod
    def default() -> "Config":
        return Config(
            include=DEFAULT_INCLUDES,
            ignores=default_ignores,
            global_languages=default_global_languages,
            enabled=default_enabled,
        )

    def is_file_type_supported(self, file_path: Path) -> bool:
        """Check if a file's resolved filetype is in the include list.

        Uses the three-layer filetype mapper (extension → filename → shebang)
        to resolve the canonical filetype, then checks against config.include.
        """
        filetype = resolve_filetype(file_path)
        if filetype is None:
            return False
        return filetype in self.include


def load_config(yaml_text: str) -> Config:
    raw = yaml.safe_load(yaml_text)

    _enabled = raw.get("enabled") if raw.get("enabled") is not None else default_enabled
    _include = raw.get("include") or DEFAULT_INCLUDES
    # Normalize: convert raw extensions (js, ts, yml, fish) to canonical filetypes
    _include = _normalize_includes(_include)

    return Config(
        ignores=raw.get("ignores") or default_ignores,
        include=_include,
        global_languages=raw.get("global_languages") or default_global_languages,
        enabled=_enabled,
    )
PYEOF
echo "config.py updated with normalization""}

{"command_line":"cat > ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/rag/lsp/config.py << 'PYEOF'
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
import os
from pathlib import Path

import yaml

from lsp.filetypes import (
    DEFAULT_INCLUDES,
    EXTENSION_TO_FILETYPE,
    FILENAME_TO_FILETYPE,
    get_filetype_for_extension,
    resolve_filetype,
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Config
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

default_ignores: list[str] = []
default_global_languages: list[str] = []  # no defaults b/c if you don't set it, you get all indexed file types (includes)
default_enabled: bool = True


def _normalize_includes(raw_includes: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    """Normalize include list: convert raw extensions to canonical filetypes.

    Handles both old-style raw extensions (js, ts, yml, fish) and
    new-style canonical filetypes (javascript, typescript, yaml, shell).
    Unmapped extensions pass through as-is. Deduplicates results.
    """
    seen = set()
    normalized = []
    for item in raw_includes:
        ext = item.lstrip(".").lower()
        canonical = get_filetype_for_extension(ext)
        if canonical not in seen:
            seen.add(canonical)
            normalized.append(canonical)
    return normalized


@dataclass
class Config:
    ignores: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    include: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    global_languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=default_global_languages)
    enabled: bool = field(default=default_enabled)

    @staticmethod
    def default() -> "Config":
        return Config(
            include=DEFAULT_INCLUDES,
            ignores=default_ignores,
            global_languages=default_global_languages,
            enabled=default_enabled,
        )

    def is_file_type_supported(self, file_path: Path) -> bool:
        """Check if a file's resolved filetype is in the include list.

        Uses the three-layer filetype mapper (extension → filename → shebang)
        to resolve the canonical filetype, then checks against config.include.
        """
        filetype = resolve_filetype(file_path)
        if filetype is None:
            return False
        return filetype in self.include


def load_config(yaml_text: str) -> Config:
    raw = yaml.safe_load(yaml_text)

    _enabled = raw.get("enabled") if raw.get("enabled") is not None else default_enabled
    _include = raw.get("include") or DEFAULT_INCLUDES
    # Normalize: convert raw extensions (js, ts, yml, fish) to canonical filetypes
    _include = _normalize_includes(_include)

    return Config(
        ignores=raw.get("ignores") or default_ignores,
        include=_include,
        global_languages=raw.get("global_languages") or default_global_languages,
        enabled=_enabled,
    )
PYEOF
echo "config.py updated with dedup""}

{"command_line":"cd ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim && git commit --author="qwen3.6-35b-a3b<wes.mcclure+qwen3.6-35b-a3b@gmail.com>" -m "feat: replace extension-based indexing with canonical filetype mapping

Introduce a three-layer filetype resolution system for RAG indexing:

1. Extension → canonical filetype mapping
   (yml/yaml → yaml, sh/fish/zsh/bash → shell, etc.)

2. Filename lookup for extensionless files
   (Makefile → make, fish_history → yaml, Dockerfile → docker)

3. Shebang fallback for extensionless scripts
   (#!/usr/bin/env python3 → py, #!/bin/bash → shell)

Key changes:
- New filetypes.py module with 58 comprehensive tests
- Config.include now uses canonical filetypes (yaml, shell, javascript, etc.)
- Backward compatible: old-style raw extensions (yml, fish, js) are auto-normalized
- Indexer discovers extensionless files via fd + filetype resolution
- Storage.for_file() resolves files to datasets using the three-layer mapper
- Validator compares filetypes instead of raw extensions
- File queue handles extensionless files through the mapper

Results:
- yaml and yml files merge into a single 'yaml' index
- sh/fish/zsh/bash files merge into a single 'shell' index
- Extensionless files (Makefile, deploy scripts, fish_history) are indexed
- All 58 filetype tests pass
- All 5 indexer tests pass
- Semantic grep queries work across filetypes""}

messages.content:
Done! Here's the summary:

## What was built

### New file: `lsp/filetypes.py` (554 lines)
Three-layer filetype resolution system with **58 tests**:

1. **Extension mapping** — `yml/yaml → yaml`, `sh/fish/zsh/bash → shell`, `cc/cpp/hpp → cpp`, etc.
2. **Filename lookup** — `Makefile → make`, `fish_history → yaml`, `Dockerfile → docker`, `.gitignore → diff`
3. **Shebang detection** — `#!/usr/bin/env python3 → py`, `#!/bin/bash → shell`, handles versioned interpreters like `python3.11`

### Updated files:

| File | Changes |
|---|---|
| `config.py` | `DEFAULT_INCLUDES` uses canonical filetypes; `load_config()` auto-normalizes old raw extensions (`js → javascript`, `fish → shell`) |
| `indexer.py` | Iterates over filetypes; `_build_fd_glob_for_filetype()` builds multi-extension globs; `get_files_diff()` discovers extensionless files via shebang/filename lookup |
| `storage.py` | `Datasets.for_file()` uses `resolve_filetype()` for three-layer resolution; `RAGDataset.filetype` replaces `language_extension` |
| `file_queue.py` | Removed hard skip for extensionless files; uses `resolve_filetype()` instead |
| `validate.py` | Compares filetypes instead of raw extensions |

### Results

- **yaml/yml** → single `yaml/` index (merged)
- **sh/fish/zsh/bash** → single `shell/` index (merged)  
- **Extensionless files** (Makefile, deploy scripts, fish_history) now indexed
- All 58 filetype tests pass ✓
- All 5 indexer tests pass ✓
- Semantic grep queries work across filetypes ✓

function.name: delegate

recursion_limit.description: LangChain's max messages for the subagent before stopping. Each subagent tool call produces 2 messages (input + output). Leave unset unless the task needs more or fewer than the default (50).

description.description: describe the task for the agent to perform

agent_type.description: which subagent profile to use, options: assistant, web-researcher, command-runner, file-finder, test-finder

function.description: Delegate to a subagent to perform relevent tasks and summarize findings.

function.name: count
function.description: Count to, for testing progress and cancellation

function.name: run_process

argv.description: Executable mode: directly spawn a process. argv[0] is the executable, followed by arguments passed verbatim (no shell interpretation). Cannot be combined with 'command_line'.

timeout_ms.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds, defaults to 30,000ms

stdin_text.description: Optional text written to STDIN (written fully, then closed). Useful for heredoc-style input or file contents.

command_line.description: Shell mode: a shell command line executed via the system's default shell. Supports pipes, redirects, globbing. Cannot be combined with 'argv'.

function.description: Run a process on this darwin machine

function.name: fetch

max_chars.description: Maximum number of characters to return

start_index.description: Start at this character offset, useful if a previous fetch was truncated and more context is required

raw.description: Do not convert HTML to markdown

function.description: Fetch a URL and extract HTML contents as markdown

function.name: run_in_neovim

lua.description: Lua code to execute. Should be a valid Lua chunk.

function.description: Execute Lua code in the current Neovim process (that hosts your agent). For example, call vim.cmd()

function.name: semantic_grep

filetype.description: limit matches to a vim compatible filetype. default includes all filetypes

embed_top_k.description: number of embeddings matches to consider for reranking

top_k.description: number of matches, defaults to 5 which is usually plenty!

instruct.description: instructions for the query, explain the type of query

query.description: query text, what you are looking for

function.description: Retrieval tool (the R in RAG) for code and documents in the current workspace. Uses a vector store with embeddings of the entire codebase. And a re-ranker for sorting results.

class IncrementalRAGIndexer:

    def __init__(
        self,
        dot_rag_dir: Path,
        source_code_dir: Path,
        options: RAGChunkerOptions,
        program_args: ProgramArgs,
        config: Config,
    ):
        self.options = options
        self.dot_rag_dir = Path(dot_rag_dir)
        self.source_code_dir = Path(source_code_dir)
        self.program_args = program_args
        self.config = config
        # TODO pass root_path instead of using fs (IF I even need it  here anymore... was it just here for ignores?)

    async def main(self):
        if not self.config.enabled:
            logger.warn(f"RAG indexing disabled in {self.source_code_dir / '.rag.yaml'}, ")
            return

        included = self.config.include
        if self.program_args and self.program_args.only_extension:
            included = [self.program_args.only_extension]
            logger.info(f"Indexing only extension: {included[0]}")

        for file_extension in included:
            await self.build_index(file_extension)
        self.flag_unindexed_extensions_with_file_counts(included)
        self.trash_vestigial_extension_indexes(set(included))
        await signal_hotpath_done_in_background()

    def trash_vestigial_extension_indexes(self, configured_extensions: set[str]):
        rag_dir_dirs = [p for p in self.dot_rag_dir.iterdir() if p.is_dir()]
        indexed_extensions = {d.name for d in rag_dir_dirs}
        vestigial_extensions = indexed_extensions - configured_extensions

        if not any(vestigial_extensions):
            return

        # logger.info(f'configured:\n  {sorted(configured_extensions)}')
        # logger.info(f'indexed:\n  {sorted(indexed_extensions)}')
        logger.warn(f'FYI found {vestigial_extensions=}, removing...')

        for name in vestigial_extensions:
            extension_dir = self.dot_rag_dir / name
            logger.warn(f"Removing vestigial rag dir: {extension_dir}")
            trash_dir(extension_dir)

    def flag_unindexed_extensions_with_file_counts(self, index_languages: list[str]):

        cmd = ["fish", "-c", f"fd . {self.source_code_dir} --exclude='\\.ctags\\.d' --exclude='\\.rag' --exec basename"]
        logger.debug("warn cmd", cmd)
        result = subprocess.run(
            cmd,
            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
            text=True,
            check=STOP_ON_FAILURE,
        )
        basenames = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()

        ignore_files = ["ctags", "ctags.d"]

        extensions = [basename.split(".")[-1] \
            for basename in basenames \
            if basename and "." in basename \
            and basename not in ignore_files
        ]

        import itertools
        unindexed_extensions = [(ext, len(list(group))) \
            for ext, group in itertools.groupby(sorted(extensions)) \
            if ext not in index_languages
        ]

        # TODO pair with an ignore style file for what not to index in a given repo

        # require at least 2 of a file before warning
        #   within one file you don't need RAG for context :)
        noteworthy_extensions = [ \
            f"{ext}={count}" \
            for ext, count in unindexed_extensions \
            if count > 1
        ]

        if noteworthy_extensions:
            logger.debug(f"Found unindexed extensions: {' '.join(noteworthy_extensions)}")

    def get_files_diff(self, language_extension: str, prior_stat_by_path: dict[str, FileStat]) -> FilesDiff:
        """Split files into: changed (added/updated), unchagned, deleted"""

        # PRN add in gitignore detection, right now I am using fd so I s/b mostly fine, still might want explicit checks here too
        #   use whatever I come up with from LS's text document events to filter... i.e. files in a .venv that I open (F12)
        #    though that again isn't an issue for this part of indexing
        # PRN add .ask.config or similar w/ ignore section to block things like manual_prompting folder! in ask-openai repo!

        # * current files
        cmd = ["fd", f".*\\.{language_extension}$", str(self.source_code_dir), "--absolute-path", "--type", "f"]
        logger.debug("current files cmd", cmd)
        result = subprocess.run(
            cmd,
            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
            text=True,
            check=True,
        )
        current_path_strs = set(result.stdout.strip().splitlines())

        # * ignored files
        ignored_path_strs: Set[str] = set()
        for path_str in current_path_strs:
            if is_ignored_allchecks(path_str, self.config, self.source_code_dir):
                ignored_path_strs.add(path_str)
        if (len(ignored_path_strs) > 0):
            logger.info(f"Ignoring files ({len(ignored_path_strs)}):\n    {'\n    '.join(ignored_path_strs)}")
        current_path_strs -= ignored_path_strs

        # * added, modified (aka changed)
        changed_paths: Set[Path] = set()
        for file_path_str in current_path_strs:
            file_path = Path(file_path_str)
            is_new_file = file_path_str not in prior_stat_by_path
            if is_new_file:
                changed_paths.add(file_path)
                logger.debug(f"[green]New file: {file_path}")
            else:
                current_mod_time = file_path.stat().st_mtime
                prior_mod_time = prior_stat_by_path[file_path_str].mtime
                if current_mod_time > prior_mod_time:
                    changed_paths.add(file_path)
                    logger.debug(f"[blue]Modified file: {file_path}")

        prior_path_strs: Set[str] = set(prior_stat_by_path.keys())

        # * deleted
        deleted_path_strs = prior_path_strs - current_path_strs
        for deleted_file in deleted_path_strs:
            logger.debug(f"[red]Deleted file: {deleted_file}")

        # * not changed
        changed_path_strs = set(str(f) for f in changed_paths)
        not_changed_path_strs = prior_path_strs - changed_path_strs - deleted_path_strs

        return FilesDiff(changed_paths, deleted_path_strs, not_changed_path_strs)

    async def update_faiss_index_incrementally(
        self,
        index: Optional[faiss.Index],
        not_changed_chunks_by_file: dict[str, list[Chunk]],
        updated_chunks_by_file: dict[str, list[Chunk]],
    ) -> faiss.Index:
        """Update FAISS index incrementally using IndexIDMap"""

        # Create base index if it doesn't exist
        if index is None:
            shape = await get_shape()
            # 768 for "intfloat/e5-base-v2"
            # 1024 for Qwen3
            base_index = faiss.IndexFlatIP(shape)
            index = faiss.IndexIDMap(base_index)
            # FYI if someone deletes the vectors file... this won't recreate it if stat still exists...

        new_chunks: list[Chunk] = []
        new_faiss_ids: list[int] = []
        for file_chunks in updated_chunks_by_file.values():
            for chunk in file_chunks:
                new_chunks.append(chunk)
                new_faiss_ids.append(chunk.faiss_id)

        # FYI FIX THE updated check logic... don't try to work around it here
        #  fix it so a chunk that is VERBATIM same content is NOT marked updated just b/c another part of the file is... OR just b/c timestamp on file changes!
        keep_ids = []  # why was I keeping things that are marked updated?!
        logger.pp_debug("keep_ids - before", keep_ids)
        for _, file_chunks in not_changed_chunks_by_file.items():
            for chunk in file_chunks:
                keep_ids.append(chunk.faiss_id)
        logger.pp_debug("keep_ids - after", keep_ids)

        # BUG: currently updated files, the chunks that don't change (or entire thing if just msec updated)...
        #   these chunks IDs are put into keep_ids AND chunks go into new_chunks
        #   and so we insert them again (double them up)
        keep_selector = faiss.IDSelectorArray(np.array(keep_ids, dtype="int64"))
        not_keep_selector = faiss.IDSelectorNot(keep_selector)
        index.remove_ids(not_keep_selector)

        if new_chunks:
            logger.pp_debug("new_faiss_ids", new_faiss_ids)

            with logger.timer("Encode new vectors"):
                passages = [chunk.text for chunk in new_chunks]
                vecs_np = await encode_passages(passages)

            faiss_ids_np = np.array(new_faiss_ids, dtype="int64")

            index.add_with_ids(vecs_np, faiss_ids_np)

        return index

    async def build_index(self, language_extension: str = "lua"):
        """Build or update the RAG index incrementally"""

        prior = load_prior_data(self.dot_rag_dir, language_extension)

        paths = self.get_files_diff(language_extension, prior.stat_by_path)

        # TODO add test to assert delete last file is fine and wipes the data set

        logger.pp_debug("paths", paths)

        if not paths.changed and not paths.deleted:
            logger.debug("[green]No changes detected, index is up to date!")
            return

        all_stat_by_path = {path_str: prior.stat_by_path[path_str] for path_str in paths.not_changed}
        not_changed_chunks_by_file = {path_str: prior.chunks_by_file[path_str] for path_str in paths.not_changed}
        logger.info(f'{len(paths.changed)} changed, {len(paths.deleted)} deleted')

        updated_chunks_by_file: dict[str, list[Chunk]] = {}
        for file_path in paths.changed:
            file_path_str = str(file_path)

            stat = get_file_stat(file_path)
            all_stat_by_path[file_path_str] = stat

            # Create new chunks for this file
            chunks = build_chunks_from_file(file_path, stat.hash, self.options)
            updated_chunks_by_file[file_path_str] = chunks

        logger.pp_debug("Deleted chunks", paths.deleted)
        logger.pp_debug("Updated chunks", updated_chunks_by_file)
        logger.pp_debug("NOT changed chunks", not_changed_chunks_by_file)

        # * Incrementally update the FAISS index
        if paths.changed or paths.deleted:
            index = await self.update_faiss_index_incrementally(
                prior.index,
                not_changed_chunks_by_file,
                updated_chunks_by_file,
            )
        else:
            index = prior.index

        if index is None:
            return

        # Save everything
        index_dir = self.dot_rag_dir / language_extension
        index_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True, parents=True)

        faiss.write_index(index, str(index_dir / "vectors.index"))

        logger.pp_debug("ids: ", prior.index_view.ids)

        with logger.timer("Save chunks"):
            all_chunks_by_file = not_changed_chunks_by_file.copy()
            all_chunks_by_file.update(updated_chunks_by_file)
            logger.pp_debug("all_chunks_by_file", all_chunks_by_file)
            logger.pp_debug("all_stat_by_path", all_stat_by_path)

        with logger.timer("Save chunks"):
            write_json(all_chunks_by_file, index_dir / "chunks.json")

        with logger.timer("Save file stats"):
            write_json(all_stat_by_path, index_dir / "files.json")

        logger.debug(f"[green]Index updated successfully!")
        if paths.changed:
            logger.debug(f"[green]Processed {len(paths.changed)} changed files")
        if paths.deleted:
            logger.debug(f"[green]Removed {len(paths.deleted)} deleted files")

{"content":[{"name":"EXIT_CODE","type":"text","text":"0"},{"name":"STDOUT","type":"text","text":"from dataclasses import dataclass, field\nimport os\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport yaml\n\n# map aliased/alternative extensions to a primary extension\nALIASED_EXTENSIONS: dict[str, str] = {\n    # used: preferred\n    \"yml\": \"yaml\",\n}\n\n# TODO I need to create groupings of related extensions... i.e. fish+zsh+bash+sh as 'shell' type\n#   TODO! combine alt file extensions that are the same: yaml/yml\n#   PRN also use shebang when chunking files? and look at plaintext, extensionless files w/ a shebang (esp chmod +x files)\ndefault_includes: list[str] = [\n        \"lua\", \"py\", \"java\", \"js\", \"ts\", \"html\",\n        \"md\", \"json\", \"yaml\", \"yml\",\n        \"fish\", \"zsh\", \"sh\", # shells\n        \"cpp\", \"cc\", \"c\", \"h\", \"hpp\", # c related\n        \"m\", \"mm\", # objective c\n        \"cu\", \"cuh\", \"cl\", # GPU\n        \"rs\",\n        \"go\",\n        \"patch\",\n    ] # yapf: disable\n\ndefault_ignores: list[str] = []\ndefault_global_languages: list[str] = []  # no defaults b/c if you don't set it, you get all indexed file types (includes)\ndefault_enabled: bool = True\n\n@dataclass\nclass Config:\n    ignores: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n    include: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n    global_languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n    enabled: bool = field(default=default_enabled)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def default() -> \"Config\":\n        return Config(\n            include=default_includes,\n            ignores=default_ignores,\n            global_languages=default_global_languages,\n            enabled=default_enabled,\n        )\n\n    def is_file_type_supported(self, file_path: Path) -> bool:\n        ext = file_path.suffix.lstrip('.').lower()\n        # TODO is this file extension or was filetype vim filetype? if so you'll have to use a lookup!\n        #   FYI can fix that later, just get this started for now so I don't log false positives in LSP server for Unsupported files (most of the time anyways)\n        file_type = ALIASED_EXTENSIONS.get(ext, ext)\n        return file_type in self.include\n\ndef load_config(yaml_text: str) -> Config:\n    raw = yaml.safe_load(yaml_text)\n\n    _enabled = raw.get(\"enabled\") if raw.get(\"enabled\") is not None else default_enabled\n\n    return Config(\n        ignores=raw.get(\"ignores\") or default_ignores,\n        include=raw.get(\"include\") or default_includes,\n        global_languages=raw.get(\"global_languages\") or default_global_languages,\n        enabled=_enabled,\n    )\n"}]}

{"content":[{"name":"EXIT_CODE","type":"text","text":"0"},{"name":"STDOUT","type":"text","text":"import hashlib\nimport json\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom qwen3 model load blowing up)\n#   also might be related to OpenMP error\nimport faiss\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom numpy.typing import NDArray\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel\n\nfrom lsp.logs import get_logger\nfrom lsp import fs\nfrom lsp.inference.client.embedder import encode_passages\n\nlogger = get_logger(__name__)\n\ndef chunk_id_for(file_path: Path, chunk_type: str, start_line_base0: int, end_line_base0: int, file_hash: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Generate unique chunk ID based on file path, chunk index, and file hash\"\"\"\n    chunk_str = f\"{file_path}:{chunk_type}:{start_line_base0}-{end_line_base0}:{file_hash}\"\n    return hashlib.sha256(chunk_str.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]\n\ndef chunk_id_with_columns_for(file_path: Path, chunk_type: str, start_line_base0: int, start_column_base0: int, end_line_base0: int, end_column_base0: Optional[int], file_hash: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Generate unique chunk ID based on file path, chunk index, and file hash\"\"\"\n    chunk_str = f\"{file_path}:{chunk_type}:{start_line_base0},{start_column_base0}:{end_line_base0},{end_column_base0}:{file_hash}\"\n    return hashlib.sha256(chunk_str.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]\n\ndef chunk_id_to_faiss_id(chunk_id: str) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Convert chunk ID to FAISS ID (signed int64)\"\"\"\n    # Convert hex string directly to int and mask for signed int64\n    hash_int = int(chunk_id, 16)  # hex => int\n    # Mask to fit in signed int64 (0x7FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF = 2^63 - 1)\n    return hash_int & 0x7FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF\n\nclass FileStat(BaseModel):\n    mtime: float\n    size: int\n    hash: str\n    path: str\n\n# BTW (str, Enum) == (StrEnum) => str is for [de]serilialization\nclass ChunkType(StrEnum):\n    LINES = \"lines\"\n    UNCOVERED_CODE = \"uncovered\"\n    TREESITTER = \"ts\"\n\nclass Chunk(BaseModel):\n    id: str\n    id_int: str  # mostly store this for comparing manually (when reviewing the files themselves)\n    text: str\n    file: str\n\n    # explicit storage of 0 indexed positions\n    start_line0: int\n    start_column0: Optional[int]\n    end_line0: int\n    end_column0: Optional[int]  # None means last column of end_line (i.e. line based chunking)\n\n    type: ChunkType\n    file_hash: str\n\n    # sig is two-fold: for re-ranker and for telescope picker results list\n    # prefer one-liner\n    signature: str = \"\"\n\n    @property\n    def faiss_id(self):\n        # return int(self.id_int)\n        # for now just recompute and skip id_int:\n        return chunk_id_to_faiss_id(self.id)\n\n    # Position views\n    @property\n    def base0(self) -> \"_Pos\":\n        return _Pos(self, 0)  # 0-based\n\n    @property\n    def base1(self) -> \"_Pos\":\n        return _Pos(self, 1)  # 1-based\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)\nclass _Pos:\n    _chunk: Chunk\n    base: int  # 0 or 1\n\n    @property\n    def start_line(self) -> int:\n        return self._chunk.start_line0 + self.base\n\n    @property\n    def end_line(self) -> int:\n        return self._chunk.end_line0 + self.base\n\n    @property\n    def start_column(self) -> int | None:\n        if self._chunk.start_column0 is None:\n            return None\n        return self._chunk.start_column0 + self.base\n\n    @property\n    def end_column(self) -> int | None:\n        if self._chunk.end_column0 is None:\n            return None\n        return self._chunk.end_column0 + self.base\n\n# * FAISS type hint wrappers\nclass Int64VectorIndex(Protocol):\n    \"\"\" Type hint wrapper for FAISS/swigfaiss runtime types so I can get some decent completions in dev \"\"\"\n\n    def __len__(self) -> int:\n        ...\n\nInt64Vector = NDArray[np.int64]\n\nclass FaissIndexView:\n    \"\"\" Concrete wrapper around faiss index type, provide better typing AND hide inner workings\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, dataset: \"RAGDataset\"):\n        self.dataset = dataset\n\n    def num_vectors(self) -> int:\n        return self.dataset.index.ntotal\n\n    @property\n    def ids(self) -> Int64Vector | None:\n        if hasattr(self.dataset.index, 'id_map'):\n            return cast(Int64Vector, faiss.vector_to_array(self.dataset.index.id_map))\n        else:\n            # TODO does this happen when creating a brand new index (not loading from file)\n            #  if so, how do I want to handle this? return empty?\n            #  AND, is this ever set? or is id_map tied to the loaded file and not what I add in memory?!\n            return None\n\n    def _check_for_duplicate_ids(self) -> Iterable[tuple[np.int64, int]]:\n        \"\"\" NOTE: there should NOT be any duplicates \"\"\"\n        _ids = self.ids\n        if _ids is None:\n            # TODO throw or warn? or use empty list?\n            return\n\n        ids, counts = np.unique(_ids, return_counts=True)\n        for index, id in enumerate(ids):\n            # this yucky approach to zip was just to shut up pyright without a bunch of other BS casts\n            id = ids[index]\n            count = int(counts[index])\n            yield (id, count)\n\n@dataclass\nclass RAGDataset:\n\n    def __init__(self, language_extension, chunks_by_file, files_by_path, index):\n        self.language_extension = language_extension\n        self.chunks_by_file = chunks_by_file\n        self.stat_by_path = files_by_path\n        self.index = index\n        self.index_view = FaissIndexView(self)\n\n    language_extension: str\n    chunks_by_file: dict[str, list[Chunk]]\n    stat_by_path: dict[str, FileStat]\n    index: faiss.Index\n    index_view: FaissIndexView\n\n    def num_chunks(self) -> int:\n        return sum(len(chunks) for chunks in self.chunks_by_file.values())\n\n    def num_files(self) -> int:\n        return len(self.stat_by_path)\n\n    def num_vectors(self) -> int:\n        return self.index.ntotal if self.index is not None else 0\n\n@dataclass\nclass Datasets:\n    all_datasets: dict[str, RAGDataset]\n    # FYI must use default_factory else the default dict {} is shared among all instances! b/c defaults are evaluated ONCE in scope that declares this class\n    _chunks_by_faiss_id: dict[int, Chunk] = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def __post_init__(self):\n        for dataset in self.all_datasets.values():\n            for _, chunks in dataset.chunks_by_file.items():\n                for chunk in chunks:\n                    faiss_id = chunk.faiss_id\n                    self._chunks_by_faiss_id[faiss_id] = chunk\n\n    def get_indexed_extensions(self) -> set[str]:\n        return set(self.all_datasets.keys())\n\n    def get_chunk_by_faiss_id(self, faiss_id) -> Optional[Chunk]:\n        # now consumers have no knowledge of the cache\n        #  this will help with updates too, to not let the updater have to think about this\n        # FYI I don't have to remove items here b/c they are content hashed so doesn't matter if they're left behind (for now)\n        # PRN might wanna move this to the dataset level to update it on updates to a doc in a dataset\n        return self._chunks_by_faiss_id.get(faiss_id)\n\n    def for_file(self, file_path: str | Path | None = None, vim_filetype: str | None = None):\n        language_extension = vim_filetype  # use if no file_path passed\n        if file_path is not None:\n            language_extension = Path(file_path).suffix.removeprefix('.') or vim_filetype\n        if language_extension == '' or language_extension is None:\n            logger.error(\"No file suffix and no vim_filetype, can't find dataset!\")\n            return None\n\n        return self.all_datasets.get(language_extension)\n\n    async def update_file(self, file_path_str: str | Path, new_chunks: list[Chunk]):\n        file_path_str = str(file_path_str)  # must be str, just let people pass either\n\n        dataset = self.for_file(file_path_str)\n        if dataset is None:\n            logger.error(f\"No dataset for path: {file_path_str}\")\n            # TODO should I create it then (from scratch) as first file?\n            return\n\n        if dataset.index is None:\n            logger.error(f\"Dataset {dataset.language_extension} has no index\")\n            # TODO should I be creating it in this case?\n            #    ADD TESTS FOR THIS\n            return\n\n        # * find prior chunks (if any)\n        prior_chunks: list[Chunk] | None = None\n        if file_path_str in dataset.chunks_by_file:\n            logger.debug(f\"Prior chunks exist for {fs.get_loggable_path(file_path_str)}\")\n            prior_chunks = dataset.chunks_by_file[file_path_str]\n\n        if not prior_chunks:\n            logger.debug(f\"No prior_chunks\")\n            prior_chunks = []\n\n        # * FAISS UPDATES:\n        new_faiss_ids = [c.faiss_id for c in new_chunks]\n        prior_faiss_ids = [c.faiss_id for c in prior_chunks]\n\n        # * YES! if chunks match, skip encoding which is most expensive part!\n        if prior_faiss_ids == new_faiss_ids:\n            logger.debug(f\"prior_chunks match new_chunks, SKIP re-encoding!\")\n            return\n\n        # * useful troubleshooting when rebuilding (won't need this if chunks match)\n        logger.pp_debug(\"prior_chunks\", prior_chunks)\n        logger.pp_debug(\"new_chunks\", new_chunks)\n        logger.pp_debug(\"new_faiss_ids\", new_faiss_ids)\n        logger.pp_debug(\"prior_faiss_ids\", prior_faiss_ids)\n\n        prior_selector = faiss.IDSelectorArray(np.array(prior_faiss_ids, dtype=\"int64\"))\n        dataset.index.remove_ids(prior_selector)\n\n        passages = [chunk.text for chunk in new_chunks]\n        vecs_np = await encode_passages(passages)\n\n        faiss_ids_np = np.array(new_faiss_ids, dtype=\"int64\")\n\n        dataset.index.add_with_ids(vecs_np, faiss_ids_np)\n\n        # * update file's list of chunks\n        dataset.chunks_by_file[file_path_str] = new_chunks\n\n        # # * updates for cache in  _chunks_by_faiss_id\n        for prior_id in prior_faiss_ids:\n            del self._chunks_by_faiss_id[prior_id]\n        for new_chunk in new_chunks:\n            self._chunks_by_faiss_id[new_chunk.faiss_id] = new_chunk\n\ndef load_chunks_by_file(chunks_json_path: Path) -> dict[str, list[Chunk]]:\n    with open(chunks_json_path, 'r') as f:\n        chunks_by_file = {k: [Chunk(**v) for v in v] for k, v in json.load(f).items()}\n    return chunks_by_file\n\ndef load_file_stats_by_file(files_json_path: Path):\n    with open(files_json_path, 'r') as f:\n        files_by_path = {k: FileStat(**v) for k, v in json.load(f).items()}\n        return files_by_path\n\ndef load_prior_data(dot_rag_dir: Path, language_extension: str) -> RAGDataset:\n    language_dir = dot_rag_dir / language_extension\n\n    vectors_index_path = language_dir / \"vectors.index\"\n    index = None\n    if vectors_index_path.exists():\n        try:\n            index = faiss.read_index(str(vectors_index_path))\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.exception(\"Warning: Could not load existing index\")\n    else:\n        logger.info(f\"No vectors.index: {vectors_index_path}\")\n\n    chunks_json_path = language_dir / \"chunks.json\"\n    chunks_by_file: dict[str, list[Chunk]] = {}\n\n    if chunks_json_path.exists():\n        try:\n            chunks_by_file = load_chunks_by_file(chunks_json_path)\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.exception(f\"Warning: Could not load existing chunks: {e}\")\n    else:\n        logger.info(f\"No chunks.json: {chunks_json_path}\")\n\n    files_json_path = language_dir / \"files.json\"\n    files_by_path = {}\n    if files_json_path.exists():\n        try:\n            files_by_path = load_file_stats_by_file(files_json_path)\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.exception(f\"Warning: Could not load file stats {e}\")\n    else:\n        logger.info(f\"No files.json: {files_json_path}\")\n\n    dataset = RAGDataset(language_extension, chunks_by_file, files_by_path, index)\n\n    num_chunks = dataset.num_chunks()\n    log_num_vectors = dataset.num_vectors()\n    logger.info(f\"Loaded {language_extension} - {dataset.num_files()} file stats, {log_num_vectors} FAISS vectors, {num_chunks} chunks\")\n    if num_chunks != (log_num_vectors or 0):\n        logger.error(f\"Num chunks ({num_chunks}) != Num vectors ({log_num_vectors}) which suggests problems with FAISS index vectors or otherwise, use rag_validate_index to check\")\n\n    return dataset\n\ndef find_language_dirs(dot_rag_dir: Path) -> list[Path]:\n    dot_rag_dir = Path(dot_rag_dir)\n    if not dot_rag_dir.exists():\n        raise ValueError(f\"{dot_rag_dir=} does not exist\")\n    if not dot_rag_dir.is_dir():\n        raise ValueError(f\"{dot_rag_dir=} is not a directory\")\n\n    return [p for p in Path(dot_rag_dir).glob(\"*\") if p.is_dir()]\n\ndef load_all_datasets(dot_rag_dir: Path) -> Datasets:\n    dot_rag_dir = Path(dot_rag_dir)\n    language_dirs = find_language_dirs(dot_rag_dir)\n    datasets = {}\n    total_chunks = 0\n    total_vectors = 0\n    total_files = 0\n    for lang_dir in language_dirs:\n        language_extension = lang_dir.name\n        dataset = load_prior_data(dot_rag_dir, language_extension)\n        datasets[language_extension] = dataset\n        total_chunks += dataset.num_chunks()\n        total_vectors += dataset.num_vectors()\n        total_files += dataset.num_files()\n\n    logger.info(f\"Loaded all datasets - {total_files} total files, {total_vectors} total FAISS vectors, {total_chunks} total chunks\")\n    return Datasets(datasets)\n"}]}

{"content":[{"name":"EXIT_CODE","type":"text","text":"0"},{"name":"STDOUT","type":"text","text":"\"\"\"MCP server for semantic_grep queries.\n\nWraps existing rag/semantic_grep functionality in an MCP tool so it can be\ncalled from any MCP client (not just inside neovim).\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport asyncio\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom mcp.server import Server\nfrom mcp.server.stdio import stdio_server\nfrom mcp.types import TextContent, Tool\nfrom rich.console import Console\nfrom rich.logging import RichHandler\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# XDG-compliant logging setup (same pattern as delegate.py)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n_xdg_state = Path(\n    os.environ.get(\"XDG_STATE_HOME\", str(Path.home() / \".local\" / \"state\"))\n)\n_log_dir = _xdg_state / \"mcp-servers\"\n_log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n_log_file = open(_log_dir / \"semantic-grep.log\", \"a\")\n_console = Console(file=_log_file, force_terminal=True, width=150, tab_size=4)\n\n_rich_handler = RichHandler(\n    markup=True,\n    rich_tracebacks=True,\n    console=_console,\n    show_path=False,\n    show_time=False,\n)\n\nlogging.basicConfig(\n    level=logging.INFO,\n    format=\"%(asctime)s %(name)s: %(message)s\",\n    datefmt=\"[%X]\",\n    handlers=[_rich_handler],\n    force=True,\n)\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Path setup — add the rag root to sys.path so lsp.* imports resolve\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n_RAG_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nif str(_RAG_ROOT) not in sys.path:\n    sys.path.insert(0, str(_RAG_ROOT))\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Import existing rag / semantic_grep infrastructure\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\nfrom lsp.storage import Datasets, load_all_datasets\nfrom lsp.inference.client.retrieval import (\n    LSPRankedMatch,\n    LSPSemanticGrepRequest,\n    semantic_grep as _semantic_grep,\n)\nfrom lsp.fs import relative_to_workspace, set_root_dir\nfrom lsp.logs import get_logger\n\nlogger: logging.Logger = get_logger(__name__)\n# logging.getLogger(\"mcp\").setLevel(logging.DEBUG)  # MCP SDK logs\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Globals — datasets are loaded once on startup\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n_datasets: Datasets | None = None\n_dot_rag_dir: Path | None = None\n\n\ndef _get_datasets() -> Datasets:\n    \"\"\"Return loaded datasets, raising if they haven't been initialized.\"\"\"\n    global _datasets\n    if _datasets is None:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Datasets not initialized — is the server started with a valid root_dir?\")\n    return _datasets\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# MCP Tool definition\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nSEMANTIC_GREP_TOOL = Tool(\n    name=\"semantic_grep\",\n    description=(\n        \"Search codebase embeddings for semantically relevant code chunks. \"\n        \"Returns ranked matches with file paths, line numbers, and content.\"\n    ),\n    inputSchema={\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"properties\": {\n            \"query\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\",\n                \"description\": \"The natural-language or keyword query to search for.\",\n            },\n            \"current_file_absolute_path\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\",\n                \"description\": (\n                    \"Absolute path of the current file (used to determine which language \"\n                    \"dataset to search). Pass empty string or omit for global search.\"\n                ),\n            },\n            \"vim_filetype\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\",\n                \"description\": (\n                    \"Vim filetype fallback when current_file_absolute_path is not provided \"\n                    \"(e.g. 'lua', 'py', 'ts').\"\n                ),\n            },\n            \"languages\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\",\n                \"enum\": [\"GLOBAL\", \"EVERYTHING\"],\n                \"description\": (\n                    \"Search scope. 'GLOBAL' searches only configured global_languages from \"\n                    \"rag.yaml. 'EVERYTHING' searches all indexed languages.\"\n                ),\n            },\n            \"skip_same_file\": {\n                \"type\": \"boolean\",\n                \"description\": \"When true, exclude matches from the same file as current_file_absolute_path.\",\n            },\n            \"top_k\": {\n                \"type\": \"integer\",\n                \"description\": \"Number of final results to return after reranking. Default: 5.\",\n            },\n            \"embed_top_k\": {\n                \"type\": \"integer\",\n                \"description\": (\n                    \"Number of candidate chunks to retrieve before reranking. \"\n                    \"Defaults to top_k if not specified.\"\n                ),\n            },\n            \"instruct\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\",\n                \"description\": (\n                    \"Instructions for the embedding/reranker model. Must be specific to the \"\n                    \"query type — this is required.\"\n                ),\n            },\n        },\n        \"required\": [\"query\", \"instruct\"],\n    },\n)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Helper — serialize LSPRankedMatch to a dict for MCP output\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef _match_to_text_content(match: LSPRankedMatch) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Convert a LSPRankedMatch into a serializable dict for MCP TextContent.\"\"\"\n    file_rel = relative_to_workspace(match.file)\n    file_path = match.file  # absolute path\n\n    return {\n        \"id\": match.id,\n        \"rerank_rank\": match.rerank_rank,\n        \"rerank_score\": match.rerank_score,\n        \"embed_score\": match.embed_score,\n        \"embed_rank\": match.embed_rank,\n        \"file\": file_path,\n        \"file_relative\": str(file_rel),\n        \"start_line\": match.start_line_base0 + 1,\n        \"end_line\": match.end_line_base0 + 1,\n        \"signature\": match.signature,\n        \"type\": match.type,\n        \"text\": match.text,\n    }\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Tool implementation\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync def handle_semantic_grep(\n    query: str,\n    current_file_absolute_path: str | None = None,\n    vim_filetype: str | None = None,\n    languages: str = \"\",\n    skip_same_file: bool = False,\n    top_k: int = 5,\n    embed_top_k: int | None = None,\n    instruct: str | None = None,\n) -> list[TextContent]:\n    \"\"\"Execute a semantic_grep query and return formatted results.\"\"\"\n    # Validate inputs\n    if not query or len(query) == 0:\n        raise ValueError(\"query is required and cannot be empty\")\n\n    if not instruct:\n        raise ValueError(\"instruct is required — must be specific to the query type\")\n\n    if languages not in (\"\", \"GLOBAL\", \"EVERYTHING\"):\n        raise ValueError(f\"languages must be one of: '', 'GLOBAL', 'EVERYTHING', got '{languages}'\")\n\n    if top_k < 1:\n        raise ValueError(\"top_k must be >= 1\")\n\n    datasets = _get_datasets()\n\n    # Build the request object using existing types\n    request = LSPSemanticGrepRequest(\n        query=query,\n        currentFileAbsolutePath=current_file_absolute_path,\n        vimFiletype=vim_filetype,\n        instruct=instruct,\n        skipSameFile=skip_same_file,\n        topK=top_k,\n        embedTopK=embed_top_k,\n        languages=languages,\n    )\n\n    # Execute the semantic_grep query (reuse existing function)\n    matches: list[LSPRankedMatch] = await _semantic_grep(\n        args=request,\n        datasets=datasets,\n    )\n\n    if not matches:\n        return [\n            TextContent(\n                type=\"text\",\n                text=\"No matches found for the given query.\",\n            )\n        ]\n\n    # Serialize matches\n    result_dicts = [_match_to_text_content(m) for m in matches]\n\n    # Format as a single readable response\n    output_lines: list[str] = [f\"Found {len(matches)} match(es):\\n\"]\n    for match_data in result_dicts:\n        output_lines.append(\n            f\"  [{match_data['rerank_rank']}]\"\n            f\" rerank={match_data['rerank_score']:.4f}\"\n            f\" embed={match_data['embed_score']:.4f}\"\n            f\"  {match_data['file_relative']}\"\n            f\"  L{match_data['start_line']}-{match_data['end_line']}\"\n            f\"\\n    {match_data['text'][:200]}\"\n        )\n        output_lines.append(\"\")\n\n    return [TextContent(type=\"text\", text=\"\\n\".join(output_lines))]\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# MCP Server\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync def serve(root_dir: str | Path | None = None) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Start the MCP semantic_grep server.\"\"\"\n    server = Server(\"semantic-grep\")\n\n    # * Initialize datasets\n    global _datasets, _dot_rag_dir\n    if root_dir is None:\n        # Try to find a .rag dir from CWD\n        cwd = Path.cwd()\n        if (cwd / \".rag\").exists():\n            root_dir = cwd\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"root_dir is required — pass it from the MCP client or run from a workspace with a .rag dir\"\n            )\n\n    root_dir_path = Path(root_dir)\n    await set_root_dir(root_dir_path)\n    _dot_rag_dir = root_dir_path / \".rag\"\n\n    logger.info(f\"Loading datasets from {_dot_rag_dir}\")\n    _datasets = load_all_datasets(_dot_rag_dir)\n    logger.info(f\"Loaded {_datasets.all_datasets.keys()} datasets\")\n\n    @server.list_tools()\n    async def list_tools() -> list[Tool]:\n        return [SEMANTIC_GREP_TOOL]\n\n    @server.call_tool()\n    async def call_tool(requested_tool: str, arguments: dict[str, Any]) -> list[TextContent]:\n        try:\n            if requested_tool != SEMANTIC_GREP_TOOL.name:\n                raise ValueError(\n                    f\"Unknown tool '{requested_tool}'. Available: {SEMANTIC_GREP_TOOL.name}\"\n                )\n\n            query: str = arguments.get(\"query\", \"\")\n            current_file_path: str | None = arguments.get(\"current_file_absolute_path\") or None\n            vim_filetype: str | None = arguments.get(\"vim_filetype\") or None\n            languages: str = arguments.get(\"languages\", \"\") or \"\"\n            skip_same_file: bool = arguments.get(\"skip_same_file\", False)\n            top_k: int = int(arguments.get(\"top_k\", 5))\n            embed_top_k: int | None = arguments.get(\"embed_top_k\")\n            instruct: str | None = arguments.get(\"instruct\")\n\n            return await handle_semantic_grep(\n                query=query,\n                current_file_absolute_path=current_file_path,\n                vim_filetype=vim_filetype,\n                languages=languages,\n                skip_same_file=skip_same_file,\n                top_k=top_k,\n                embed_top_k=embed_top_k,\n                instruct=instruct,\n            )\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(\"semantic_grep request was cancelled\")\n            raise\n        except ValueError as error:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid semantic_grep arguments: {error}\")\n            raise RuntimeError(str(error)) from error\n        except Exception as error:\n            logger.exception(f\"Unexpected error in semantic_grep tool\")\n            raise RuntimeError(str(error)) from error\n\n    # * Start the server\n    options = server.create_initialization_options()\n    async with stdio_server() as (read_stream, write_stream):\n        await server.run(read_stream, write_stream, options, raise_exceptions=False)\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    \"\"\"Entry point — parse root_dir from argv or use CWD.\"\"\"\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n        description=\"MCP server for semantic_grep queries\"\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--root-dir\",\n        type=str,\n        default=None,\n        help=\"Root directory of the workspace containing a .rag directory\",\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    try:\n        asyncio.run(serve(root_dir=args.root_dir))\n    except Exception as error:\n        logger.exception(f\"[bold red]Server error:[/bold red] {error}\")\n        raise\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    # for python3 -m mcp_server\n    # entrypoint doesn't use this, b/c it uses importlib (hence why you setup mcp_server.__main__:main to invoke main() there too)\n    main()\n"}]}
